description: §8.1.1 soundness-bypass close (S264 adversarial-review item 1) — a `?{}` body whose only literal content is a SQL comment (`?{`/* dynamic */${q}`}`) is STILL a runtime expression: the comment is not validatable or param-bindable SQL, and the emit is the runtime-assembled `_scrml_sql`/* dynamic */${q}`` injection vector. `sqlBodyIsRuntimeExpr` strips SQL comments (`--` to end-of-line and slash-star block comments) from the outside-of-interpolation text BEFORE the no-literal-SQL test, so a leading/trailing comment cannot cloak an all-interpolation body. Fires E-SQL-003.
rationale: §8.1.1: `The ?{} template body SHALL remain a literal string at compile time (E-SQL-003)`. A SQL comment is not literal SQL — it carries no query the compiler can validate or bind. A body that is comment(s) + whitespace + `${...}` interpolation(s), with no real SQL keyword, is exactly the forbidden runtime-expression form; a comment prefix must not defeat the check. Resolution: write literal SQL inside the backticks and bind values via `${...}`, or extract a function for reuse (§8.4.1).
